input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
# .NET MAUI Development Setup on macOS

## 1. Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | Compilers, git, etc. |
| **.NET SDK (latest LTS or STS)** | [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) | Runtime + CLI |
| **.NET MAUI workload** | Installed via `dotnet workload install maui` | MAUI templates & build targets |
| **JDK 17** | Included via MAUI workload or [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download) | Android build toolchain |
| **Android SDK** | Auto-provisioned by MAUI workload, or via Android Studio | Emulator, platform APIs |

## 2. Installation Order

```bash
# Step 1: Install Xcode from App Store, then accept license
sudo xcodebuild -license accept
xcode-select --install

# Step 2: Install .NET SDK (use the installer from dot.net)
# Verify:
dotnet --version

# Step 3: Install the MAUI workload (handles Android SDK + JDK)
dotnet workload install maui

# Step 4 (optional): Install Android Studio for emulator management
# https://developer.android.com/studio
```

## 3. Discovering the Right Versions

```bash
# See available .NET SDK versions:
dotnet --list-sdks

# See installed workloads and check for updates:
dotnet workload list
dotnet workload update

# See which Android/iOS platforms are available:
dotnet workload search
```

Check the official support matrix at:  
[learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms)

## 4. Verification

```bash
# Create and build a test app
dotnet new maui -n TestApp
cd TestApp

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet run -f net9.0-ios

# Run on Android emulator (must be running)
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

## 5. Key Notes

- **Xcode version matters** — each .NET MAUI release supports specific Xcode versions. Check the [MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/whats-new/).
- The `maui` workload auto-provisions Android SDK and OpenJDK — you usually don't need to install them separately.
- For a richer IDE experience, use **Visual Studio Code** with the [.NET MAUI extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui) or **JetBrains Rider**.
- Run `dotnet workload update` periodically to stay current.
